generic_rules: Focus repairs on resolving unresolved identifiers or invalid helper declarations reported by the AGREE validator.
When fixing type errors, ensure helper declaration types match expression types (bool, int, real).
Prioritize repairs that use only visible AADL features or locally declared helper variables.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `eq` to define helper variables with explicit types.
Use `int`, `real`, and `bool` for AGREE scalar type declarations.
Ensure binary expressions (`<=`, `>=`, `and`, etc.) have operands of the same type.
anti_patterns: Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers not present in the model.
Do not attempt to repair by modifying unrelated architecture.
Do not add architecture to resolve AGREE errors; focus on fixing the contract.
irrelevant_topics: AADL scope rules for component types and implementations
AGREE assumption/guarantee placement in AADL scopes
AGREE logical operators and branching logic
